rationale: This structure organizes the vast field of Brain & Cognitive Sciences into 12 subspecialties that reflect distinct research communities and their primary levels of analysis. The design balances traditional divisions in neuroscience (Systems, Cognitive, Computational) with major fields in psychology (Cognitive, Developmental, Clinical). I deliberately combined closely related areas like 'Social & Affective Neuroscience' and 'Cognitive Neurology & Neuropsychology' to create more robust categories and improve the MECE property. Foundational topics like 'Learning & Memory' and 'Perception & Action' are included as distinct categories because they represent large, coherent research communities that cut across multiple methodologies, providing a natural home for papers focused on these core processes.

subspecialties.name: Cognitive Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: The neural substrates of the human mind: perception, memory, and attention.
covers: The brain basis of mental processes in healthy humans
fMRI, EEG, MEG, TMS, and non-invasive brain stimulation studies
explicitly_excludes: Purely behavioral studies without neuroimaging → Cognitive Psychology
Invasive animal models → Systems & Behavioral Neuroscience

subspecialties.name: Systems & Behavioral Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: Neural circuits, animal models, and the biological basis of behavior.
covers: How ensembles of neurons and circuits generate functions and behaviors
Animal models, electrophysiology, optogenetics, circuit tracing, calcium imaging
explicitly_excludes: Purely molecular or cellular mechanisms → Biology
Human cognition studies using non-invasive methods → Cognitive Neuroscience

subspecialties.name: Clinical Psychology & Psychopathology
subspecialties.tagline: Understanding the cognitive and neural basis of mental disorders.
covers: Cognitive, behavioral, and neural mechanisms of psychopathology
Anxiety, depression, psychosis, personality disorders, psychotherapy research
explicitly_excludes: Pharmacological treatments → Medicine
Cognitive deficits from acquired brain injury → Cognitive Neurology & Neuropsychology

subspecialties.name: Cognitive Psychology
subspecialties.tagline: The architecture of the mind: information processing, models, and behavior.
covers: Models of mental processes like attention, memory, and reasoning
Behavioral experiments, reaction times, eye-tracking, computational cognitive modeling
explicitly_excludes: Direct measurement of brain activity → Cognitive Neuroscience
The influence of social context on behavior → Social & Affective Neuroscience

subspecialties.name: Developmental Science
subspecialties.tagline: How the brain, mind, and behavior change across the human lifespan.
covers: Cognitive, social, and neural development from infancy to aging
Longitudinal studies, infant cognition, adolescent brain development, typical and atypical development
explicitly_excludes: Diagnosis and treatment of developmental disorders → Clinical Psychology & Psychopathology
Focus on adult steady-state function → Cognitive Psychology or Cognitive Neuroscience

subspecialties.name: Social & Affective Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: The neural basis of emotion, motivation, and social interaction.
covers: How the brain processes emotions, empathy, morality, and social cues
Neurobiology of mood, stress, social cognition, theory of mind, intergroup relations
explicitly_excludes: Non-social cognition → Cognitive Neuroscience
Purely behavioral social psychology → often outside this L1

subspecialties.name: Computational & Theoretical Neuroscience
subspecialties.tagline: Mathematical and computational models of the brain and cognition.
covers: Modeling neural systems from single neurons to large-scale networks and behavior
Information theory, reinforcement learning, dynamical systems, neural coding
explicitly_excludes: Applying existing ML models to analyze data → the relevant empirical subspecialty
Purely behavioral modeling without neural grounding → Cognitive Psychology

subspecialties.name: Cognitive Neurology & Neuropsychology
subspecialties.tagline: The impact of brain injury and neurological disease on cognition.
covers: Cognitive and behavioral syndromes from stroke, dementia, TBI, and neurodegeneration
Patient-based cognitive assessment, lesion-symptom mapping, rehabilitation
explicitly_excludes: Mental disorders without clear neurological origin → Clinical Psychology & Psychopathology
Basic brain function in healthy subjects → Cognitive Neuroscience

subspecialties.name: Language Science
subspecialties.tagline: The cognitive and neural basis of language and communication.
covers: Language acquisition, comprehension, production, and representation
Psycholinguistics, neurolinguistics, speech perception, sign language
explicitly_excludes: Formal linguistics without cognitive/neural data → Humanities
Natural Language Processing (NLP) → Computer Science

subspecialties.name: Perception & Action
subspecialties.tagline: How we sense the world and interact with it through movement.
covers: Sensory processing (vision, audition, touch), motor control, and sensorimotor integration
Psychophysics, motor learning, multisensory integration, embodied cognition
explicitly_excludes: Higher-level reasoning about perceptual input → Cognitive Psychology
Cellular mechanisms of sensory neurons → Biology

subspecialties.name: Learning & Memory
subspecialties.tagline: How experience shapes the brain and guides future behavior.
covers: Mechanisms of memory encoding, consolidation, and retrieval
Episodic memory, working memory, skill learning, fear conditioning, synaptic plasticity
explicitly_excludes: Learning in a primarily social context → Social & Affective Neuroscience
Developmental changes in learning across the lifespan → Developmental Science

subspecialties.name: Decision Science & Neuroeconomics
subspecialties.tagline: How the brain makes choices, evaluates risk, and represents value.
covers: Neural mechanisms of choice, reward processing, and valuation
Reinforcement learning models, game theory, intertemporal choice, foraging
explicitly_excludes: Decision-making in a purely social context → Social & Affective Neuroscience
General learning mechanisms not tied to value → Learning & Memory

uncovered_concerns: Consciousness Studies was considered but excluded as a separate category due to its small audience size and significant overlap with Cognitive Neuroscience, Psychology, and Computational Neuroscience.
Neuroimaging (fMRI, EEG) was not made a category as it is a methodology used across many subspecialties rather than a distinct field of inquiry.
Educational Psychology and Industrial-Organizational (I-O) Psychology were excluded as they are highly applied fields with distinct literature bases often falling outside this category's scope.
